threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Overground' instead of 'the Chocolate Line' like the locals do. Get ready for some confused looks on the platform.
Last night on the 196, a guy in a parka kept trying to bargain with the driver for a free ride. Never seen that before.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in full lederhosen ordering a döner at 3 AM, I realized Neukölln really is a living tapestry of surprises.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a bar that serves currywurst cocktails—spicy, tangy, and a total conversation starter.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their casual dismissal of BART delays—while newcomers are still looking for updates on their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy fixing his sax on the bus, played a sweet tune just as the doors opened. It was like a mini concert before the chaos outside.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just stroll through Parc Güell without realizing it’s often packed and the locals avoid it like the plague.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their bewildered look trying to navigate the Gràcia street art while fumbling with a paper map instead of using Google Maps.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it’s cool to walk your dog without a coat in winter. Everyone else’s pup is sporting a stylish fleece by now.
Heard a busker on the 50 last night belting out old Amy Winehouse - crowd went wild, felt like we were at a Brixton gig instead of stuck in traffic.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted an old lady knitting by the bus stop while a group of kids were breakdancing next to her. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how people here will cheer when the tram finally arrives on a snow day? It's like a mini-celebration for public transport!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their reaction to BART delays; they roll their eyes, while newcomers look genuinely confused and start checking Google Maps.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the bus stop with a ukulele while a pigeon danced nearby. Only in SF does rush hour feel like a street performance.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists miss that the morning mercados are just as vibrant as La Boqueria but way more authentic. Try the ones on Travessera!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they still struggle with the 'll' sound when ordering at a café—it's like hearing them try to speak a secret language.
